notes: Maps C++ objects to RDBMS; reduces build time 30%; introduced 1991. Planned Java support by end 1996.
Runtime product enabling live object caching; limitation: one client per cache in 1996.
Planned for late 1996; would allow multiple clients per shared object cache.
Dominant data persistence layer; Persistence Object Builder maps C++ objects to relational tables.
Failed to gain commercial traction per Aberdeen; relegated to narrow markets.
Persistence planned Java support by end 1996; Java later incorporated Persistence tech via EJB.
Primary language for Persistence Object Builder at time of study.
Required for distributed OO applications with Persistence; Iona Orbix, SunSoft NEO, Visigenic, Expersoft supported.
Persistence supports Encina, TopEnd, Tuxedo; provides transaction coordination.
